item.title: Learn C on Linux
item.description: Came across this article of ways to Learn C on Linux which took me back to my 3rd real programming job. It was with a software house mainly working with C/C++ on Linux/ Unix and embedded systems. My first real experience use C/C++ in a professional environment, happy days with a good bunch of geeks. If you’ve never tried it before it would be a good change from more recent languages to learn C for a challenge.

item.title: 6502 Assembly Language
item.description: Been trying to relearn 6502 assembly language for fun, its a lot faster and easier than the first time on actual hardware when I was a teenager. Been using a book I picked up from eBay Discovering BBC Micro Machine Code by A.P. Stephenson Using BeebAsm assembler on my Mac with a BBC Micro emulator, rather than build in BBC BASIC one. You can find my code at Github https://github.com/alastairhm/beebasm

item.title: Acorn Electron Sound Mod
item.description: Interest video about modding the Acorn Electron.

item.title: Cubes
item.description: After watching Speed Cubers documentary on NetFlix decided to start back trying to learn to solve the cube from memory rather than following the instructions. So far I can get upto the middle layer without refering to the online walk through instructions. Cubes

item.title: Fidget
item.description: With WFH since March I have been finding myself fidgetting more while on video calls. Usually with a lump of Blu-Tak (other options are available), but got this delivered yesterday to keep my fingers busy.
